dialogueLvl1.text: Hi, Zack, what are you doing?
The camping stove is broken, so I'm trying to fix it. Do you want to help?
I don't think it's a good idea.
Why not ? If you don't know I can teach you.
It's more that I risk breaking it further...
Fine, could you give me the spare gas canister then?
Sure thing, here you go!
Did you actually just bend the canister by grabbing it ?
Sorry !

dialogueLvl2.text: Zack, do you really need to be that careless?
What do you mean?
The way just rush at enemies, only to dodge at the last second! it's way too dangerous.
It would be dangerous if I actually get hit!
Come on, don't play dumb. What if you tripped? Or what if someone attacked you from behind?
That won't happen.
How do you know?
You always have my back, right?
Well I try to, but you don't make it easy!
Then let's both keep fighting exactly the way we do.
Please don't.

dialogueLvl3.text: Zack, do you have a minute? I think I have a problem.
What's going on ?
You remember when you asked me if I wanted to help you fix stuff ?
Yeah.
Well, I wanted to help, so I tried to study the camping stove...
Right.
And I may have dismantled it to see what it's like inside.
And you don't know how to assemble it back.
Precisely. I'm sorry.
Nah, it's alright. Give me the pieces.
Here they are.
Lea, do you know what "screws" are ?
Aren't they the same as nails ?
Almost, except you are not supposed to pull them.
But, they were so loose I barely had to pull.
Good thing we have a spare one.
I'm so sorry.
Next time you want to learn, just tell me. We'll do it together.

dialogueLvl4.text: Hi, Lea. Are you free right now?
Sure!
Here, I made you something.
Really? For me? What is it?
It's a full toolbox. You have been making lots of progress with your tinkering, so I thought you might need your own tools.
It's so nice of you, I don't know what to say.
Just say thanks.
But... What's the occasion?
You don't need any special occasions to give something to the one you love, right ?
The one... You love?
Lea? You alright? She fainted...

dialogueLvl1.text: Hey, Zack. Still slacking off?
You spend too much time with Sorak, man.
Come on, you don't have to keep pretending to be lazy.
I am lazy, though.
You're not kidding anyone. We know you pretended to be a bad student to avoid the draft.
Not even old man Sorak?
At any rate, I've seen you train in your garage.
You never know when you might get in trouble, right? I wouldn't want to be a burden. Especially not to you and Lea.
You are literally the opposite of a burden, you keep pushing us around...
I am not the team leader?
Then stop slacking of and start training, leader.

dialogueLvl2.text: Ha! Take this!
That's not how you should be holding your spear, you know?
Zack! You startled me.
Why are you fighting with your hand. Be both know you're better at telekinesis.
Last battle, I almost hit Lea. I tried helping her, and I almost hit her with my spear.
I saw. But what are you saying, you didn't miss. Without you she would have been dead.
It was too close. Next time, I might not be so lucky.
Why don't you train your telekinesis, then?
It's less precise. I want to try wielding my spear with my own hands.
With those scrawny arms?
I'm being serious, Zack.
And so am I. Lea can protect her front, and leave her back to me. Focus on taking down the enemies far from us and we'll all be ok.
Yeah, you're right. You want to train with me?
Hell no, it's nap time!

dialogueLvl3.text: I knew it! You're actually training.
Well, it happens from time to time. I don't want to end up a dead man.
You should tell Sorak, you know. It might ease the tension between the two of you.
Let him think what he wants. I don't really care. It's also way funnier this way.
Come on. You know he's not that dumb.
Isn't he?
Stop screwing around, man. You really think your little scheme worked, back in the village?
well, duh.
But it didn't.
What do you mean?
It was all Lyv. She vouched for you. The soldier from Occidens wanted you to be drafted. He wanted to put some discipline in you.
I... I never knew.
You don't need to invent clever schemes and to try and solve everything yourself.
You're right. I'm sorry.
Just remember you are not alone, leader. We're with you, now and always.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hi Sorak, how's your day?
It just got a whole lot worse.
That's sad. Is there anything I can do to make it better?
If you stopped being so obnoxious it would be a great help.
You had to ask for the only thing out of my grasp...
Stop it. What do you want ? Or did you come here just to make fun of me?
No, as a matter of fact I wanted to check up on you. You look even grumpier than usual when you're by yourself.
You came to check up on me? You?
Incredible, right?
Listen, I appreciate the gesture, but next time don't joke around, ok?
Sure thing, my man. I hate it when my underlings mop around!

dialogueLvl2.text: What's up, Sorak?
I don't have time for you. Just say your piece and leave.
I've been wondering. Why don't you like me?
Are you really asking me that? Do you even hear how you talk to me.
No, that's not what I meant. Even when I was a kid, you've always hated me. I wasn't badmouthing you back then, so why?
Because you've always been the worst. You're talented, and that is why. I know that even though you never trained with us, you still trained on your own.
However, no one short of a genius can master so many different Blade types in such a short time. I only know one person capable of wielding so many different Blades.
So what ?
I'm not done. While you're an insult to hard work, that's not the real reason I hate you. You can do what you want, as long as you take responsibility.
The thing is, you never took responsibility. Elwyn and Lea have always covered for you. Always. They had to work even more because you were to lazy to play according to the rules.
Listen, I know I screwed up, ok? What can I do to make up for it?

Nothing, Zack. I'll keep following you and obeying your orders because it's what Sir Mir told me to do, but I am not and will never be your friend. If you really want to make up for it, do it with your friends.

dialogueLvl3.text: Zack-Sorak 3

dialogueLvl1.text: Hello, Zack. Do you need any help with the cooking?
Hi, Lyv. Sure, you can help. Could you watch the pot while I cut the vegetables?
Of course.
Thanks a lot. I appreciate it!
I wonder, can you teach me how this device works?
Which device? The camping stove?
Yes. Sorak always cooks, so I have never used one before.
Ever since I've been a kid, you taught me so much. It's nice to be the other way around for once.
I remember you used to spend a lot of time with us back then.
Yeah, Granpa was always busy.
It takes me back...
Hold on, what's that burnt smell?
I may have forgotten about the pot.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hey, Lyv. So it's your turn to cook today?
Yep! The others went hunting and brought back some meat.
Are you making some kind of stew ?
Precisely. I'll try to make Sorak's stew.
Did he give you his recipe ?
No but I've seen him make it so many times I know it by heart.
Do you need a hand ?
Sure, could you cut up the vegetables? They are over there.
You got it. I can't wait. It has been so long since I last ate it.
Yeah... It's a bit of a shame that you stopped eating with us.
It would have been hard to ditch class if I ate with the teachers. Sorak would have probably refused to feed me...
Maybe so, but you would have deserved it! You shouldn't stay locked up in your room. Young boys and girls should play around together outside!
I should have asked Elwyn and Lea to ditch classes with me...
No, that's not what I meant!
Is Sorak's stew usually this black?
NOOOOOOOOOO.

dialogueLvl3.text: Zack, you're holding your arm. Are you alright ?
It's just a bruise from the last battle. It hurts but it's really nothing.
Show me. I'll heal you.
It's fine. Don't bother.
Don't be stupid and let me heal you! What will you do if it gets infected ?
Ok, ok.
Here, all better. Be more careful next time, please.
Thanks a lot.
Say, Zack. Why did you choose to become a Hunter ? Elwyn grew up in the school. Lyv has a past with pluvials. But you ?
I don't really know, it sort of happened. I spent a lot of time with Elwyn and Lyv, so when they asked me if I wanted to be part of their squad, I just accepted.
But you stopped following classes barely a few months after starting. Why didn't you choose another way.
Well I couldn't leave them hanging now, could I? Who knows what kind of trouble they would have gotten in without me.
I don't think they would have been in more trouble than now...
You may have a point.
It's alright, as long as you're here for them and they're here for you.
Don't worry, I'm here for you too!
Likewise, Zack.

dialogueLvl1.text: Sieg, I need your help !
What? You need my help? Really?
Yeah, I figured you must be pretty knowledgeable about Old World tech.
Yes, I am an archaeologist after all.
Ok, so I found, this thing in a half buried ruin. Do you know what this is ?
I do ! It was used for cooking bread. I think it was called a "toaster".
Cooking bread? Isn't bread already baked?
Yes, but in the Old World there was a custom which was to cut slices of bread and cook them a second time.
What? But why not just make thinner loaves of bread?
Well, according to books, they hated crust. They even sometimes cut the crust before selling it.
You could buy bread without crust?! It truly was the peak of civilisation.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hi, Zack. Are you free ?
Sure thing. What can I do for you, Sieg ?
Well, I have a question. How do you manage to fight ?
Usually, I just summon my Blade and I start hitting stuff with it.
No, that's not what I meant. How do you keep your cool while fighting? Whenever an enemy gets close to me, I start to freak out.
So that's why your specialty is bows.
Yes, it's so that I can be as far from the enemies as possible.
You say you freak out, but I have seen you fight. Even when you get hurt, you're even fiercer than usual!
But I'm scared of getting hurt, Zack! That's why I use a bow!
Yeah, of course. Sorry. How about this: if you're ever in real trouble just call me and I'll cover you.
I wouldn't endanger you for my sake!
It's alright, danger is my middle name. Just call me, ok ?
If you say so...

dialogueLvl3.text: Sieg, are you ok?
It's not as bad as it looks, don't worry. The others already did all the healing they could. I just need a bit of rest.
I'm sorry man, you got hurt because of me.
Don't apologize. It's not like I'm dying. Also, when I saw you were in danger, I knew I could get overthrown by fear. The fear of losing you was way worse than the fear of getting hurt.
I see. Thanks for the save, Sieg. I don't know if I would be there now without you.
You're welcome. I know you have my back, but I also want you to know that I have yours.
Of course I know that, you idiot. Now, look what I found.
Some kind of tin can ?
Yeah, it says on the label that it's canned bread! Do you want to try it out ?
Sure.
Damn it's true. It doesn't have crust.
It's awful, though.
Yeah, it's easily the worst bread I've eaten in my entire life.

dialogueLvl1.text: Elwyn, I'm looking for Zack. Have you seen him?
No, sorry. Do you want any help?
Yeah. He's been missing for a while. I'm starting to get worried.
Do you think something happened to him?
I hope not... We have to find him. For all we know he could be all alone surrounded by pluvials.
I'll go ask the others.
Already have... No one has seen him. What if he is already...
Calm down. he can probably look after himself. It's Zack we're talking about. When we were kids it was him who found us when we were lost in the forest, remember?
Yeah, you were crying so loud he heard us.
Hold on, do you hear that?
Could it be snoring?
Damn it, he is napping on that tree !

dialogueLvl2.text: Thanks for earlier, Lea. You really saved my bacon.
It's alright. I was only able to do it because you got that enemy when I was getting overwhelmed.
Don't mention it.
We're really efficient when we're together, we should fight like this more often.
Yeah. We really compensate for each other's weaknesses.
If you want, I could teach you how to use shields.
I don't know how to summon a shield Blade, though.
Well, you could just take a real, tangible shield?
I mean... It doesn't hurt to try, I guess.
Ok, let's train with this pot lid. Hold it firmly and try to defend against my punch.
Are you sure about this?
Here I go!
OOF.
It sent him flying. Elwyn, are you alright?

dialogueLvl3.text: Hi Elwyn, still training? It's pretty late.
Really? Time flies.
The others just finished eating. I brought our portions. Let's eat.
Thanks a lot. I'm starving.
Say, don't you think you train a bit too much? You must rest from time to time or you won't last...
I can't. If I neglect my training, I could die. Even worse, one of you could die.
Don't you worry about me. You know how sturdy I am!
I know, but still. I don't want to see you hurt.
Neither do I, but don't you want to call it a day?
Yeah, maybe you're right. I had something I wanted to ask Zack anyway.
Next time you want to train, come find me, alright? We'll do it together.
Sure! I'd be glad to do it with you.

dialogueLvl4.text: Elwyn! Ready for our training session?
Sure, but first I have something to say.
What is it?
Well, you know, we have been together ever since childhood.
Yeah?
And you know that you and Zack are my best friends. No, you're more than that. You're both family to me.
Keep going.
But you, Lea, you're even more than that to me. You're the most important person in the whole world. And... That's why I want to give you this.
A ring? You took your sweet time, you dummy.
Lea, would you be my wife.
Of course, yes! I'll be your forever.
Forever and ever.

dialogueLvl1.text: Sorak, do you have a minute?
Of course, Lea, what can I do for you?
You have been fighting pluvials for a long time, right?
Yes for some years, now. Although it was mostly the weak pluvials that dared to attack the village. Why do you ask?
Well, I am struggling a bit when we fight in the rain. I mean, it gets muddy and whenever I try to block an attack I'm always close to losing my footing.
I see what you mean. The answer is simple, really. Pluvials usually act on instincts from their past life. They will usually either blindly follow you or never move from where they are.
In the first case, you should lure them outside the rain, on rocky ground for example. In the second case, just rely on Elwyn's telekinesis, or the other idiot's bow.
I get it. But what do you do when they don't want to target you? What I usually do is just ram them to make they focus on me.
I mean, another valid strategy is putting the enemy of their misery before having to block anything.
I'll tell Elwyn and Zack to do just that.
Just tell Elwyn, the other idiot would just rush head on.
Noted.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hi Sorak, what's up?
Zack has been messing up with me even more since he has become our leader. Other than that, I'm good.
I wanted to ask you something.
Of course, what is it?
Why did you choose to become a hunter?
I think you and I have the same reason. There was someone I wished to protect, and so I enrolled. It's as simple as that.
I think I know who it is.
It's obvious, isn't it. Anyway, I can say the same. It's good. We all need a reason to keep going. Protecting those you love is one of the very best there is.

dialogueLvl3.text: Lea, you really got better!
It was just a fluke.
No, you won this duel fair and square...
What's wrong Sorak? You're usually more into it.
You know what? I just realized something.
What is it?
You all grew up so much. It feels like yesterday you were a little kid and now look at you.
Come on, your embarrassing me.
Bear with it, because I won't say it twice. I know it wasn't easy, but now you're all as good as me and Lyv, maybe even better.
Sorak...
I just wanted to say... I'm proud of you all.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hi, Lea. How are you?
Lyv! You're just the person I needed.
Oh my! What can I do for you?
Can you teach me telekinesis?
Sure, but why so suddenly? I mean, I tried several times already but...
I know, I broke more windows than I can count, but being able to attack from a distance can be a real lifesaver in a brawl.
You know what? Let's try it! Here, focus and try to hit the target.
Here I go!
What the heck?!
Holy cow, you almost decapitated Zack!
How? I was behind you.
Sorry...
It's alright. Let's go over the basics.

dialogueLvl2.text: Lyv, I need a hand.
Please, Lea, if it's about the telekinesis, I can't do anything about it anymore. I am sorry to say this, but you might be a lost cause. Sorry. But it's true.
Ok that hurts a bit, but let's forget it. I need you to teach me about staves.
Oh, heavens... Don't you mean, like, greatswords? You can't hit things very well with a staff.
I know. I want to be able to heal the others. Sometimes, just protecting them with my shield is not enough.
Hmm. I see. Ok, try to summon a staff.
Ok, here it is.
It's really... Quaint?
Listen, I know it looks like an oversized twig, but it must work all the same, right?
You know what? Let's try hit. As a matter of fact, I have a small bruise from the last battle. It's nothing, so it shouldn't be hard to heal it.
Here I go!
Ouch! Did you just hit me?
I... Isn't it how you're supposed to use it?
No, not exactly. Listen, I'll go over it with you, once again...

dialogueLvl3.text: Look, Lyv, now my staff looks more than yours now! I mean, it still looks like a tree branch, but it's still a bit better!
That's some progress, well done! Zack told me you trained with him and managed to make his eczema less itchy. Well done.
Yeah, I trained for hours on end, but it all paid off!
Well, it's certainly an improvement. What is that.
Pluvials! Watch out ! I'll take them on, support me from behind.
Ok !
Take that !
Lea, be careful, that tree!
Thanks. Eat this !
Did you just hit the remaining pluvials with the tree?
Sheesh, that was the last of them. I'm a bit tired, now.
You know what, with a strength like this, you probably don't need to use anything else than you shield and your strength.
You think?
Yes. Believe in your skills, they're well enough to protect everyone. I mean, you did protect me just now.
I... Thanks. I guess it's must fun that way too!
Right, you've always liked whacking things.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hi Sorak, do you have a minute?
Of course, Elwyn, what can I do for you?
I wanted to ask you something.
Of course, what is it?
Why did you choose to become a hunter?
There is no particular reason. When I was younger, I always got into fights. In the end, it was either the hunters or the army.
And what made you choose the hunters over the army?
Let's just say that there was someone in Landfall Village who wanted to be a hunter too. It would have been lonely if I joined the army/

dialogueLvl2.text: Hi, Elwyn, how are you doing? You look a bit down.
Oh, hi Sorak. Yeah, It just feels like I'm not getting any better. I know I have been training a lot, but I just don't feel like I'm improving at all.
Nonsense. If your father could see you, he would be so proud of you.
I... I wish I could meet him.

He was the type of man you rarely meet in your life. When he died in the incident all these years ago, he made sure to save as many people as he could, you included. Each passing day, you are making him proud. I know it.
Thanks, Sorak. I really needed to hear that.

dialogueLvl3.text: Sorak, I'd like you to tell me more about the incident where my father died.
That's a sudden request, but sure. I was there, so I can tell you everything I remember.
Thanks, Sorak.
It was about when the ruins excavations was nearly concluded. Sir Mir was there. Actually, he was the one leading the whole operation.
Mir? I thought he was a hunter, not an archaeologist. Also, how old is he exactly?
I've been wondering the same thing. Anyway, just as they opened the gates of the ruins, a whole army of machines swarmed out of it.
Machines? Like the ones when we returned from the Northern Cave?
Yes, but they just kept coming and coming, and they were all over the place. Sir Mir and the most of the workers were trapped inside the ruins. He was the only survivor.
I see. So he was the one who saved everyone else, including me.

No. By the time he came out, it was over. It was mostly your father who saved as many villagers as he could. In the end, his injuries were too severe. He died with a smile on his face, knowing that you, in particular, was safe.
I wish I could have met him...
Me too. You two are really alike. He made me promise to take care of you.
Thanks, Sorak. You kept your promise. He can rest easy. I've always been in good hands.
Of course. I will always be there for you, Elwyn.

dialogueLvl1.text: What a tiring day! The others really need to be more careful.
Yeah, they might be putting a little bit too much trust in our healing abilities...
It reminds me of when Sorak and I were your age. He was always so reckless. It feels like Lea picked up on that trait of his.
Lea? She is not reckless. Most pluvials can't even make her flinch. Zack is the reckless one!
Ha ha ha. You are absolutely right. It's good that you have their back.
Without us, Zack would have probably died a hundred times already.
Probably.

dialogueLvl2.text: Good evening, Lyv. What are you doing?
Hi, Elwyn. I am updating my journal. I was just about to be done.
A journal? Why do you keep one?
No reason, really. I just like to unwind on paper. Every once in a while, I read it and it reminds me of how far we've come.
I see.
It helps you put things into perspective. With evrything that happened. It's easy to feel a bit lost. It helps me remember that there is as much good as bad in this world.
You don't have to feel lost, Lyv. We're not kids anymore, you can tell us when you're feeling down. We can help you out.
Look how much you have grown, Elwyn. Understood. Next time I feel like this, I'll come to you. Thanks.

dialogueLvl3.text: Hi Elwyn, how are you? You seem a bit down.
It's... It's nothing.
Really?
Well... You know, last time we talked, you told me about your journal. I started one too.
That's great! I'm glad you found it useful.
Well, that's the thing. I also felt like writing all that happened. It's more like a memoir, I guess.
I see, so what's the problem?
You told me writing yours helped you put things into perspective, but I just can't seem to do that. I just keep writing and recently it's been one catastrophe after another.

I see. I guess it's normal to feel that way. All we do is fighting and watch our friends get hurt all the time. It's hard to see the good in that. We can't all be happy go lucky all the time, like some idiots I know.
I guess it would be a lot easier.

Maybe so... But listen, I have an idea. Why don't you try writing about the good things that happened? They don't have to be big things, just the little things that made you smile. It can be something as simple as a nice meal or a good joke.
Like when we saw that pluvial slip in the mud and get impaled on his own spear?

Exactly! Anything that made you smile, even for a second. It can be a good memory or something that happened today. Just try to focus on the good things, and I promise you, it will help you put things into perspective.
I guess I can try that. Thanks, Lyv.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hello, Sieg. What are you up to?
I'm reading an old book I found in the ruins we visited a while ago. It's preserved impecably!
Oh, really? What is it about?
It's about the cataclysm. It's some kind of diary from a survivor. I just started it, but we could learn a lot from it, I think.
Don't we already know what happened during the cataclysm?
Well, when it happened, most people still relied on computers and stuff to document things. Most of it was lost, the rest of the archelogists usually try to recover books like this one.
I see. Do you think it could be useful for us?
I don't know yet, but I think it's worth a read. Maybe we can find some useful information in it.
I guess it is always interesting to learn more about the past. Can you tell me more about it when you finish reading it?
Will do!

dialogueLvl2.text: Lyv, Lyv! I just finished reading that book I found, and I think it's really interesting!
Oh, really? Calm down, and tell me about it.
It looks like the diary is from a survivor of the ground zero of the cataclysm, where the Beast appeared.
Landfall Village?
Yes, exactly. The diary is from someone who worked at the ruins under the village. At the time it was a research facilty. Of some sort.
So... What happened? Does it say what caused the Beast to appear?

Saddly, no. The writer was a janitor. He didn't really know anything about what happened down there. He wrote that most of the complex was destroyed in a cavein when the Beast appeared, so it's likely that the most important information was lost.
It's not anything new, then?
Not really, but it's still interesting to read about it from a first hand account!
I guess so. Thanks for sharing it with me, Sieg.
No problem! It's always nice to share interesting things with friends!

dialogueLvl3.text: Sieg! What happened? Are you alright?
It's nothing. I just go a little bit bruised trying to save a hard drive I found earlier.
Why would you do that? Your safety is more important than some old hard drive, you know. Here, let me heal that for you.
Thanks. It feels much better already.
You're welcome. So, what was on that hard drive? Was it something important?
Who knows? It's not like we have a computer to read it on, but I think it's worth trying to recover the data on it. It might be something useful.
Do you even know if it still works? I mean, it's been exposed to the elements for a long time.
Nope!
You risked your life for a hard drive that might not even work? That's so reckless!

Yes, I know. But we've lost so much knowledge about the past, and I just want to recover as much of it as I can. If even the slightest bit of information can be recovered from it, it will be worth it.
I understand your motivation, but please be more careful in the future. I don't want to lose you.
I will try to be more careful, I promise.
You're just saying that to make me feel better. "Try" is the keyword, isn't it?
Could be!

dialogueLvl1.text: Hi, Ruben. What are you up to?
Nothing much. Training a bit.
Well, this is awkward. I'll get going, then.
Hold on. I just wanted to say... Sorry.
For what?
For snapping at you when we found you. It was uncalled for.
Yeah, you were a bit of a jerk. It's alright.
Thanks. I'll leave you to your training, then.
To me, you're still a jerk, but I guess that if Elwyn trusts you, then I can give you a chance. See you around.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hey, Zack. Do you have a minute?
Sure, what's up?
I just wanted to thank you for earlier, you really saved my bacon there.
Anytime. You're a member of our crew. We look out for each other.
I know, but I just wanted to say it. I really appreciate it. Maybe... Maybe I acted like a jerk, too.
Well, then let's just call it even. How about we stop being jerks to each other from now on?
Yeah I could go for that.

dialogueLvl3.text: Take that!
You're a bit clumsy, man.
Damn it, I know. Fighting pluvials is so different from fighting people. It's so much easier...
hey, you're the one who asked to train with me, so you should be used to it by now.
I wish. You get stronger every day.
You're not so bad yourself, Zack. It's just that the way you train is a bit weird.
I guess. I have been making it up as I go along, so it's not surprising that it's a bit unorthodox.
That's what you get from skipping classes, I guess.
Sorak's classes are so boring, man! Also, I don't want to fight people. I want to fight pluvials!
Me too... I'm relieved that I didn't get to join the army...
Yeah, me too!
Thanks for saving me, back there, Zack.
No problem. Anyway, let's get back to training! This time you'll eat dirt!

dialogueLvl1.text: Ruben! It looks like the two of us are taking watch together tonight.
Seems like it. I'd rather be training, to be honest.
That's so like you. You know that people usually sleep at night, right?
I don't like sleeping. It's a waste of time.
Tell, me Ruben. Why do you train so much. I mean, until recently, you didn't even want to be a fighter.
Do you know what it's like to see your friends run around in panick while you're stricken by fear, unable to do anything but hide in the mud?
Ruben...
I was so scared that I couldn't even move. I wish I could be more like Zack or Elwyn. They're so brave and strong. The only thing I can do is train.
You're already just as brave as they are, Ruben.
Right. I'll never be weak again. Never. Whenever I close my eyes, I the amry officials who tried to fight the pluvials...
Don't worry. I know you can do it. Just remember that you have us to support you.
Thanks, Lyv. I think I'll get a few swings while we watch.

dialogueLvl2.text: Ruben, wake up! Are you alright? Ruben!
What? Where am I? I must have dozed off...
You fainted in the middle of your training. You really need to rest more, Ruben. You look exhausted.
I'm sorry, I just... I don't have time, alright?
I've had enough, Ruben. I swear to the Hero, if you don't get some rest, I will make you get some rest.
What? No it's ok, I don't need to...
Sorak? Come over here please. I need your help with something. And get some rope.
Fine, fine! I'll go to sleep! Just... Please don't tie me up!
Kids, I swear...

dialogueLvl3.text: Ruben, you look good today!
Yeah, I've been getting plenty of sleep lately. I feel great!
That's good to hear, Ruben. You look refreshed.
Yeah, I also started training with Zack, and it's been really fun. I feel like I'm getting stronger every day!
That's great, Ruben. Keep up the good work!
Thanks, for the encouragement and everything, Lyv. I really needed someone to help me out.
Even if we're not in the village anymore, it's still my job to take care of you all.
Thank you for everything, Lyv.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hi, Ruben. Have you been doing alright?
I'm fine, thanks.
Good to hear. We haven't really talked that much in a while, have we?
Yeah... Ever since you joined Zack's squad, we haven't been able to hang out as much.
Yeah, you're right. He's a handful, that one...
I have always wondered, Elwyn. Why did you choose to join Zack's squad?
Well, at first, it was because he looked so lonely. He was always the kid who was working in his machines. I was a bit curious when he volunteered to be a Squad Leader.
Really? That's it? I thought there was some kind of deeper reason behind it. Lea even followed you out of sheer loyalty.
Yeah, sorry it your desappointed. I know you're not particularly fond of him, but when you get to know him, he's actually a really nice guy. He's just a bit... Weird.
Yeah, I know, I know. I would have liked to have you in my squad, though. We would have been the best squad.
Well then, good thing we're all in the same squad, now!
That's not really what I meant, but I guess it works.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hey, Elwyn. Wanna train together?
Sure, that sounds like fun! But, huh, you know I'm not really versed into closed combat, right?
I know, I'm mainly a bow user myself. It's alright!
Okay, let's do it then!
Great! Let's go some dodging drills! I'll shoot some arrows at you and you try to dodge them!
Wait, hold on... Aaaaaagh! That hurts!
My bad! I thought you would dodge it... Are you ok.
Of course I'm not OK. You just shot me in the knee!
Oh, I'm really sorry! Don't worry, I'll take out the arrow so you can heal yourself.
Waitwaitwait. Ouch! That hurts even more! Why would you do that?
Just... Shut up and heal yourself!
I can't do that!
What? Really?
What the hell man? Is this your everyday training routine? Go get Lyv!
Yeah, something like that, except Zack usually dodges the arrows.
Oh, you try to dodge this one, you maniac!
Hold on, hold on !

dialogueLvl3.text: Hey, Elwyn. Are you ok? You look a bit... Bruised.
Yeah, well last time we trained made me realize that maybe I should try to work on my defensive skills.
Oh really?
Yeah, so I tried training with Zack, but it hasn't been going very well.
I see...
I don't really know how to get better at dodging and stuff. Usually Zack and Lea shield me.
Maybe it's just not your forte. You can't be good at everything, you know.
Yeah maybe, but depending on others is a pain. I want to be able to take care of myself.
What? That's stupid! We're fighting as a team right? So we should be able to rely on each other, right?
Yeah, I guess you're right... Hold on, why do you train your dodge-game, then? You're a bowman!
It's because I'm pretty good at about everything but telekinesis. That's your thing.
You know you're most friendly irritating guy I know, right?
Thanks, I think?

dialogueLvl4.text: Hey, Elwyn. Do you have a minute?
Ruben! Sure, do you need anything?
No, not really. I just wanted to hang out with you.
Of course! I was just about to get you.
Say, when this is all over, what do you want to do?
I don't know. Maybe return to the village and try to rebuild it? Settle down there?
I'm thinking settling down is pretty good.
If it comes down to it, would you like to settle down with me?
Yeah, I'd say that's a pretty good idea.
Great! I think we would make a great team!
Yeah, I have been thinking that from the moment I met you, to be honest. I just didn't want to say it out loud because I thought it would be weird.
It's a bit weird, but it's a good weird. I like it.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hey, Ruben. Can I talk to you for a minute?
Kira, what's up?
I was thinking about what happened when you were supposed to be drafted. That shouldn't have happened.
What are you talking about?
We were short on troops, but sending only an officer and not a whole escort to pick you up was really... It was dumb of us.
I... What are you trying to say, Kira?
Nevermind. I'll see you later.
What the hell was that?

dialogueLvl2.text: Kira! What's up? You want to do some target training together?
Sure, I was about to go practice myself.
It's always impressive to see you move your weapon around like that.
Thanks. Your bow weilding is not too shabby either, you know.
Yeah, well it's always easier to hit a stationary target than a moving one, it's way harder to shoot Zack.
What?
What?
I was just saying that it's harder to shoot Zack than a stationary target, that's all. When we're training I mean.
Oh ok, I thought you were saying something else for a second there.
Anyway, let's get back to it!
Maybe I should try to train with Zack then, he might make for a good target.

dialogueLvl3.text: Kira! Are you on cooking duty tonight?
Yeah, I'll be grilling some meat. It'll be delicious!
Can't wait to try it. Well, I'm off to do some training, I'll be back in a bit!
Wait, Ruben. Do you remember what we talked about a while ago?
About when I left Landfall Village with the others?
Exactly. I... I was the one who decided to have you guys from Landfall Village drafted.
What?

I've always been helping Dad with strategic decisions and stuff. We are really short on troops, you know? The war is at a standstill, but should enemies decide to cross the Bridge, I don't know if we would be able to hold them off.
I... Don't know what to say,
With the increase in Pluvial activity, I wanted to keep as much soldiers as possible to guard the city. I should have sent some to escort you. I'm sorry.
I knew there were lots of Pluvials around the city. I... I messed up Ruben. I'm really sorry.
You know, when Elwyn and Zack told me that the village was destroyed, I thought that maybe I could have done something to save it.
But deep inside, I know that I was too powerless to do anything. I mean, if it wasn't for you I might be dead right now.
Ruben...
This is what war is about, isn't it? Strategists make decisions that affect the lives of soldiers. I know you, Kira. It's alright.
Ruben... You're so brave. I wish I could be as brave as you.
Nah, you're brave enough. It takes guts to make that kind of decisions. Anyway, isn't your meat burning?
Noooooooooo!

dialogueLvl4.text: Kira! I've been looking for you. I wanted to talk to you about today's mission.
Sure, what's wrong.
Well, it feels like there was a flaw in you strategy. I know you plan carefully, but the way we held back compared to Zack's squad put us all in danger.
Yeah, I know Zack's a hothead, but I didn't expect him to be that reckless. It threw me off a bit, to be honest.
How about next time, we try to engage the enemy first? That way Zack'll have to follow our lead.
That's a good idea. It might be a bit hard to get Zack to follow orders, but I'll try to make it work.
Perfect! I know you can do it, Kira. You're a great strategist, and I'm sure you can make it work.
That's nice of you to say, Ruben. You know, I really like these little strategic meetings we have.
Well, you know how it is... I just like spending time with you, Kira.
Really? Spending time with me? How so?
Well... I... You know...
I'm just teasing you, Ruben. I know what you mean. I like spending time with you too. I would love to spend more time with you, actually.
What? Really? I mean... Yeah... Sure!
You're as red as a tomato, Ruben! I didn't know you were that shy!
I... I'm not shy! I just... Don't tease me alright!
Sorry, sorry. Anyway, let's get back to work! We have a lot of planning to do for the next mission!

dialogueLvl1.text: Hey, Gusto. What are you up to?
Oh, nothing much. I just woke up from a nap. Do you need anything?
Ok, so there is something I need to talk to you about.
Of course. Tell me.
I want you to teach me how to cook! Back in the capital, you always cooked for me. Now, when I get cooking duty, I have no idea what to do but grilled meat!
What's wrong with grilled meat?
That's beside the point! What if someday we can't find meat?
Then you can just grill vegetables or something.
Hold on... Do you mean that you can just grill anything?
Apart from water, yes you can.
Gusto! You're a genius!
I'm always happy to help.

dialogueLvl2.text: Hello Kira, do you have a minute?
Is it about yesterday's incident? I'm sorry...
It's alright. Anyone can make mistakes. Let's just talk about it.
I didn't know...
Sooo, I'll try to be clearer. You cannot grill anything. You can only grill food. You can't grill a rock, for example.
It's just that... When you have very hard meat, you can make it softer by cooking it for a long time. I thought that maybe if I grilled a rock for a long time, it would work.
I see... But no, that won't work. You have to pick food that is already edible first.
There wasn't enough meat for all of us, so I improvised.
Yeah but you didn't need to paint it red to make it look like meat, you know. Zack almost ate it!
I promise I won't do it again...

You know what? I have an idea. I'll teach you about a new cooking technique. It's called 'stewing'. You put some food in a pot, add water, and cook it for a long time. It makes the food really soft and delicious!
That sounds amazing! What do you put in a stew?
Well, you can put pretty much anything in a stew.
Anything?
No, wait!

dialogueLvl3.text: Hello Kira, do you have anything planned for today?
No, I'm free, why?
Well, I am cooking dinner tonight. If you want I can show you the ropes?
Sure! What are you cooking?
Well, I am making an old dish called beef strogonoff.
Is it a stew?
Not really, but kind of. It's a dish where you cook beef in a sauce made of sour cream and mushrooms. It's really delicious!
Wow! That sounds amazing! I can't wait to try it!
Ok. I prepped the beef in advance. It's better to make it marinate for a while. We can grill it a bit in advance.
What? Why do you need to grill it? I thought you were going to stew it?
Hmm, well, I like to grill the beef a bit before putting it in the sauce. It gives it a nice flavor.
So when a recipe calls for stewing, it's actually better to grill the meat first? Is everything a barbecue in disguise?
No, not everything is a barbecue in disguise. But grilling does add a nice flavor to the meat, so I like to do it before stewing.
Cooking is so complicated! I thought it was just about putting stuff in a pot and waiting for it to be done!
Wait until I tell you about steaming and frying...
What's steaming?
It's when you boil stuff but put what you are cooking in a basket above the water, so it cooks with the steam instead of being boiled. It's really good for vegetables and fish.
What the hell? I thought everything was either grilled or stewed! This is too much for me!

dialogueLvl4.text: Hello Kira, I heard you were on cooking duty tonight. Do you want some help?
No, Gusto, I got this. I want to try cooking something new tonight!
Something new? What do you have in mind?
It's a secret. You'll see when it's done!
Are you sure? I mean...
No! You'll see. You'll be impressed.
Ok I'll come back in a bit then. I want to give you some space to work on your masterpiece!
Thanks, Gusto! See you later!
There... It's done! Gusto, you want to be the first one to try it?
Hold on... Is that...
That's Gulash!
It's... It's my favourite dish...
Yeah, well I wanted to make something special for you, Gusto.
You went through all the trouble of making Gulash just for me? That's... That's so sweet...
Oh, Gusto, are you crying? I didn't mean to make you cry!
It's so nice of you...
Don't cry! Tell me how it tastes!
It's the best thing I have ever eaten. Thank you so much, Kira. I really appreciate it.
It's a special dish for a special person. I'm really glad you like it, Gusto.

dialogueLvl1.text: Sieg. Here you are! I have been looking for you.
Miss Kira, Sir, what can I do for you?
I already told you to drop the 'Miss' and 'Sir' stuff. Just call me Kira, alright?
Of course, Miss Kira. What is it about?
Nevermind... Anyway, you never debriefed your mission with me.
Oh yeah, in the Landfall Mountains. So much happened it completely slipped my mind.
Well, why don't you tell me about it now?
Well, the intel you gave me was pretty accurate. There are dozens of underground tunels under the moutains, and there are nearly no Pluvials there.
Interesting, so what happened then? We lost contact with you for a while, so I was a bit worried.
One of the tunnels led me to the underground complex under Landfall Village.
Oh, and then you lost track of time fidgeting with every single little thing in there, right?

Listen they are priceless artifacts, I had to check them out! I found some really interesting stuff in there, you know. I found some old documents and... Yeah, I guess I got a bit carried away. I'm sorry.
It's alright, Sieg. I'm just glad you're safe.
Anyway, I got trapped there, with Machines on one side and Pluvials on the other. That's when Zack and his friends found me.
Talk about being in the right place at the right time...
Yeah... It's so sad, what happened to the village.
I wish there was something we could have done to save them.
All these artifacts and documents...
I was talking about the people, Sieg. The villagers. I wish we could have done something to save them.
Oh... Yeah, them too.

dialogueLvl2.text: Kira! Kira! Look!
What is it, Sieg?
I found a really doodle in ruins earlier! I think it represents an early version of a Blade Engine!
Wasn't the first one created by the Hero and his companions, back when they were trying to kill the Beast?
It might be the case. Their journey got them to a lot of different places, so it's possible they went to the ruins at some point. I don't know, but it's really interesting!
Could we learn something from it? At the current time it's really hard to make new Blade Engines...
I'm not sure, it mainly talks about a preliminary design. I think they weren't even sure it would work at that point.
It's a shame we don't have more time to explore the ruins. There is so much interesting stuff in there, and we might be able to find something that could help us in the war.
What, are you actually interested?
Yeah, it's important to learn where we come from.
Finally someone who understands me.

dialogueLvl3.text: Hi Sieg, can I ask you a favor?
Kira! Sure, what do you need?
I was trying to go through some strategy books in order to come up with better tactics.
I'm not sure how much I can help you with that, Kira. I'm not really a strategist, you know.
I was wondering if you had any Old World books about strategy.
Actually, I did read a book like that a while ago.
Really, and do you remember anything?
Well, they fought really differently back then. After all, they didn't have to deal with Pluvials and Machines. And they couldn't weild Blades.
They must have been really weak compared to us, then...

Probably, but they relied a lot more on technology to give them weapons to fight with. They had a lot of different types of guns and stuff, so they had to come up with different strategies to use them effectively.
It's weird, when you think about it. That they had their mighty technology, but they still lost to the Beast and its Pluvials.
I looks almost unreal that such a creature was defeated by a single man. The Hero was really something else.
I wish he was still around. I'm sure he would easily stop the war...

dialogueLvl4.text: Hi Kira, I was looking for you.
Sieg! it's nice to see you. Did you find something interesting in the ruins again?
Well, about that... You probably know about the ruins nearby.
Yes! I was thinking it was a shame we could spare some time to explore them.
Well, as a matter of fact, I asked Zack and the others and they are fine with stopping for a while to gather supplies.
That's great news! I was hoping we could explore the ruins together, Sieg.
I was about to ask you just that. I would love to explore the ruins, just the two of us.
Are you asking me out on a date, Sieg?
Yeah, I am. I like you Kira!
Wow, you're much more straightforward than I expected, Sieg!
I practiced a little bit, I wanted to make sure I said it right.
I'd love to go on a date with you, Sieg.

dialogueLvl1.text: Kira, I need to talk to you.
Great, Zack, cause I need to talk to you too.
I'm glad to have you with us, but you know it's a bit weird to have two people dishing out orders.
I was thinking the exact same thing! That's why I wanted to tell you I'll be the one in charge now.
Nice, that's cool. Hold on, what?
Yeah. The one who has the most experience in battle should be the one giving orders, right? And that's me.
How come? You've never even been an apprentice hunter.
I've been training as a soldier even since I've been a kid. I have been concelling my father with strategic decisions and stuff for a long time.
I see... But...
And from what I've heard, you haven't been the most diligent student.
Ok, so how about we just lead our own squads and mind our own business?
Fine with me.
Deal.

dialogueLvl2.text: Zack, what's that thing in your chest?
Oh, this? It's a crystal.
That's it?
What do you mean?
You have a crystal in your chest and just say 'oh, this?' Like it's nothing?
I don't know, it's just something I have. I don't know what it does or anything. It's just there.
I see... So you just have a crystal in your chest and you don't even know what it does?
Pretty much.
That's really weird, Zack. You should probably get that checked out by a doctor or something.
I don't think there are any doctors around here who would be able to help me with that.
So you just keep it there?
Yeah, I guess so. It's not really bothering me or anything. It even acts as a sort of armor, so it's not all bad. It's pretty sturdy! Look...
You don't need to show me, I get it!
Your turn now. What's this weird thing that sometimes shows up behind your back?
Oh, that? It's my guardian spirit. It protects me from harm and stuff. It's really cool!
I don't know either, ok? He's just there when I need him.
I would be creeped out if I had an old man following me, to be honest. You're kinda weird.
Hey, shut up! You're the one who's half rock!
I am not half rock! I'm half crystal!
Whatever.
I guess we're both pretty weird, huh?
Yeah. You're weirder though.

dialogueLvl3.text: Zack, I've been wondering about something.
Sure, what is it?
How come we have special blades? I mean, I've always had my Reshine. What about your... Thing?
Well, it's not even a weapon. Initially it was just a tool to tinker with stuff. It's just that I can change its shape to my will, so I can use it as a weapon if I need to.
But how do you do that?
I don't know. I just can. It was a pain to learn to weild all these different weapon types, but I guess now I can do some pretty cool tricks.
You're pretty awesome, Zack. I wish I could do that.
Where did that come from? You're pretty awesome too. I'm just good at whacking things and building stuff. You got that whole strategy thing going on.
Well, you could learn if you wanted to. I could teach you if you want.
You know what? I might just take you up on that.

dialogueLvl4.text: Zack, do you have a minute?
Sure, what's up?
Well, I found something earlier. Look.
Are those... Schematics?
yeah, I found them in the ruinsof some kind of workshop. I think they are for some kind of machine or something.
It's for a bike! That's so cool!
I know, right? I thought it was really interesting. I wonder if we could build one. I read about it but I have never seen one before.
"we"? You want to help me build it?
Well, I have been trying to learn how to build stuff in my own free time.
It would be sweet to do it together!
We could build two of them! One for you and one for me!
That would be awesome! We could go everywhere!
Ok, so what do we need?
There is the list of parts here. We could use some help, let's ask...
No!
What is it?
I want to build it with you, just the two of us. I want it to be our project.
Oh... Ok, that sounds nice. Real nice.
Let's do it together.

dialogueLvl1.text: Hello, Sieg. What are you doing?
Just reading a book. It's about the creation of the bridge.
Really? I have never even thought about how the bridge was built.
Well, when you think about it, without the bridge we wouldn't even be at war.
I wonder why it was built in the first place.
Well, according to the book. It was the Hero who wanted it built to connect the two nations.
It backfired quite a bit.
That's... One way to put it.
All of this depressing talk is making me hungry. Let us grab a bite.
I'll finish my book while we eat, then.

dialogueLvl2.text: Sieg, I need your help with something.
Hi, Gusto. What can I do for you?
You are well versed in books, right?
I would not say I'm well versed, but... I know a bit about Old World books.
That is great, but what about cooking books?
Cooking books? I am not sure if I have read any of those before.
I have been looking to expand my repertoire, and I thought it would be a great idea to recreate dishes from the Old World.
That's a good idea. It's... Culinary archaeology! Let me take a look at my books and see if I can find anything.
It is ok, it is no pressing matter. I'll get back to you in a while.
You stay right there! Today, we cook for science!

dialogueLvl3.text: So, Gusto, any luck today?
I am sorry, Sieg. After all the trouble you went through to find me this book, I still don't understand.
Are these recipies really that hard to understand?
Well, they use some strange terms. What even is a "brunoise" or a "julienne"? And even the ingredients are weird. What is a "foie gras"? I have never even heard of that before!
I see... I might be able to help you out with some of that. Foie Gras is actually from an ancient language called French.
French? That is a strange name for a language. So what is this ingredient?
It means "fat liver". It's a special way of preparing liver that was considered a delicacy in the Old World.
That sounds... Disgusting! Why would anyone want to eat that?
Well, it's not for everyone, but some people really like it. It's really rich and flavorful, so it's an acquired taste.
It might be a little bit too difficult to prepare... Let's try some other dishes. What is this "Escargot"?
Escargot is snails. You know what? This recipe book sounds a bit weird.
Snails? Thats is leagues worse. I don't even want to know how you prepare that.
Maybe you should just stick to the dishes you know, Gusto.
You are right, Sieg. Instead of trying to make some fancy new dish, I will just perfect the ones I know!
I can't wait to try it, Gusto!

dialogueLvl1.text: Gusto, are you cooking something? I'm supposed to be on cooking duty tonight, just get some rest, man.
Good evening, Zack! It is alright. I enjoy cooking. I hope you don't mind.
Really? I also like to cook!
Let us do it together, then!
Ok, so what's on the menu?
You know, I was thinking something simple tonight.
Like... Fried eggs and stuff?
Precisely! You read my mind, there.
Do we have any bread and cheese? We could make some grilled cheese sandwiches to go with the eggs.
Why not. We also have some lard, we could use that to fry the eggs. It will make them taste even more delicious!
I wouldn't dream of making fried eggs without lard! Tonight is gonna be a feast!
Let us make sure of that!

dialogueLvl2.text: Zack, I am in need of assistance. Would you be willing to lend me a hand?
Sure thing, Gusto. Do you need help with cooking?
In a way. The stove broke down again.
I keep fixing this thing, but it keeps breaking down. I don't know what's wrong with it.
Oh, I see. Should I cook on the campfire then?
No, I'll fix it, don't worry.
This is a great news! I am happy to have someone as skilled as you around, Zack.
Keep the praise for when I actually fix it, Gusto. Thanks, though. There, that should do it for now.
It is impressive! You work really fast, Zack. I don't know how you do it. I am in your debt.
Nah, just cook something great and we'll be even.
Of course, Zack.
We should really buy a new stove next time we get the chance, though.
Yes, that might be a good idea.

dialogueLvl3.text: Zack! Didn't expect to see you here. Do you want us to cook together?
Nope, not tonight!
Then, why are you peeling potatoes? I don't understand.
Listen, Gusto. You always cook for us, even when you don't have too. Tonight, you just kick back.
What? I don't mind cooking, really. I enjoy it. I want to cook for you guys.
Just shut up and grab a glass, Gusto. We're having a barbecue party tonight! For you!
For me? I don't know what to say, Zack. That's really nice of you!
Don't mention it. I'm glad to have you on the team.
What can I do to help?
Man, just stop! You sit back, you shut up and you enjoy the food. That's an order!
I... Ok, I can do that. Thanks, Zack.

dialogueLvl1.text: Gwenie! how are you? Are you doing ok?
Yeah, being with you guys is great! I mean, my sister's ships are way better than yours, but it's still cool!
Your sister's ships? You mean...
Kay's pirates! She is my sister.
Are you a pirate too, then?
I was. But I prefer hanging out with you all. Being a pirate is hard work, believe me.
But then, what were you doing with Raghnall's troops?
I felt like seeing the world, you know? I wanted to go on an adventure! So I asked my sister if I could join another group.
And she put you with these bastards...
They are not that bad! When you get to know them, they are actually pretty nice. Kind of like you guys!
I'll take your words for it. They tried to kill us, though.
Not really... I mean, they were just trying to capture you all.
Is that really the orders you got? Why?
No idea! Anyway, it's time for my shift. I'll see you later, Kira!

dialogueLvl2.text: Yo ho, a pirate's life for me!
Gwenie, I thought you said you didn't want to be a pirate anymore?
I don't! I just like to sing pirate songs sometimes, you know? It's fun!
Ok, but maybe you could sing something else? Like... A song about the Hero or something?
The Hero? Who's that?
You know, the guy who defeated the Beast and built the bridge and stuff?
Oh, that guy! I know about him. He's pretty cool. I don't know any song about him, though.
Yeah, me neither. Back home, I have a whole books about him and his adventures.
We must have some of them back in Archades as well.
Hold on, you're Archadian?
Kind of. The lair of the pirates is on an island North of the Archadian continent, so I guess you could say I'm from there.
I see... I didn't know.
Well, if that's a problem for you, know that the Kay pirates plunder every side! Especially the Occidensians suckers, though.
I know that already, Gwenie. I'm the daughter of the Occidensian leader. Do you know how many lives were lost because of the Kay pirates?
You were... Then I...
Sorry. I snapped a bit. It's ok. It's not like you gave the orders or anything, Gwenie.
Huh... Right... I never had anything to do with that. Nothing! Mostly...
You're awfully red, you know that?
Me? Suspiciously red? That must be a fever, I better go!

dialogueLvl3.text: Gwenie, have you been avoiding me?
What? Me? Avoiding you? You're the one who's been avoiding me!
Come on, Gwenie. What's wrong?
Nothing! There couldn't be anything wrong! I don't feel guilty about anything, I swear!
Is this about when you were a pirate? I thought you loved being a pirate, Gwenie. Why would you feel guilty about that now?
Fine... It's just that ever since now, I thought we were the good guys, you know? Kay told us we always stole for the greater good. Now I'm not so sure.
I see... Well, now that you're with us, you'll have plenty of chances to atone for your past deeds, Gwenie. I'm sure you'll do great things with us!
Oh, Kira you're so sweet... I really want to do good things with you guys.
I'm sure you will, Gwenie. I'm sure you will.
I almost fell like I should return the trinket I stole from Zack.
What?

dialogueLvl4.text: Here, Kira, that's for you!
Is that a bandana? It's really pretty! Where did you get it?
I made it for you!
It's the same as yours! But, what's the occasion?
This bandana is the symbol of my family. I wanted you to have one too, so I made you one! I hope you like it!
Part of your family...
Why are you so red, Kira?
I'm so glad you made this for me, Gwenie. It means a lot to me.
Nice! Now when this is all over we can go on adventures together with the rest of the guys!
The rest of the guys?
Yeah! To us, our family is our pirate crew!
I see... Well... That's fine too...
But giving the bandana to someone outside of the family is a really big deal! It means you're ready to be with that person forever! I hope you know that, Kira.
What? Talking to you is really... tiring. But I'm so glad to be part of your family, Gwenie.
As of now, we're 4! You, me, Kay and her Husband! It's enough to go on an adventure!
As long as it's with you.
